--- title: "Argo OHC Sampling Bias — North Atlantic Current" subtitle: "PARCELS Simulation Study with GLORYS12 Velocity Field and OHC" author: "Daniel Ross" date: today format: html: page-layout: full toc: true toc-depth: 3 number-sections: true code-fold: true code-tools: true embed-resources: true fig-width: 9 fig-height: 6 theme: light: cosmo dark: darkly lightbox: true jupyter: python3 execute: warning: false cache: true --- ```{=html} ``` # Goal and design **Reanalysis-based simulation study** 1. Use the reanalysis **GLORYS12** for synthetic velocity and temperature data 2. Advect **virtual Argo floats** with the GLORYS velocity field using the PARCELS `virtualfleet` package 3. "Observe" the temperature profile at each float's surfacing position 4. Estimate OHC from the virtual floats within grid cells 5. Compare to the *true* OHC integrated from the full GLORYS field 6. Quantify the bias directly **This report.** Deploy a grid of virtual floats in the North Atlantic Current where there is a strong current and temperature gradient and compute bias at different grid sizes ::: {#setup .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code code-summary="Setup — imports and experiment configuration"} import sys, os sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath("../code")) import numpy as np import pandas as pd import xarray as xr import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import ohc import ohc_bias from trajplots import (open_trajectories, map_trajectories_minimal, map_positions_by_month, map_point_trajectories, plot_depth_profiles, map_field, map_fields_row) # --- experiment configuration (mirrors code/configs/nac_gs_1yr_wide.json) --- TRAJ = "../data/virtualfleet/nac_gs_1yr_wide_task*.zarr" # batch trajectory zarrs VELOCITY = "../data/velocity_gs_wide/velocity_*.nc" # GLORYS uo/vo/thetao (extended) TRUTH_BOUNDS = (30, 48, -74, -49) # advection domain (lat0, lat1, lon0, lon1) DEPLOY_BOUNDS = (36, 40, -68, -62) # float deployment box (4° lat × 6° lon) = analysis region ANALYSIS_BOUNDS = DEPLOY_BOUNDS DEGS = [1.0, 0.5, 1/12] # estimator cell sizes to compare MAP_DEG = 0.5 # cell size for the monthly maps DEPTHS = ["ohc_700", "ohc_2000"] # both depth layers, shown throughout ``` ::: # Simulation The float advection is run by `code/run_trajsim.py` with a JSON config using the `PARCELS` python package `virtualargofleet` with function `virtualargofleet.VirtualFleet.simulate` For this report I deployed one float per 1° cell (24), on the GLORYS 1/12° grid over the box lat 36 to 40°N / lon −68 to −62°W on 2020-01-01. The floats were advecting with **daily** GLORYS velocities at the 1000m parking depth for all of 2020. The simulation computation time step was set to the default of 300s and new float positions were recorded to `NetCDF` files every 6 hours. I downloaded the velocity and temperature data using the `copernicusmarine` python api from the GLORYS12V1 CMEMS product `{"dataset_id": "cmems_mod_glo_phy_my_0.083deg_P1D-m", "variables": ["uo", "vo", "thetao"], "lon_bounds": [-74, -49], "lat_bounds": [30, 48],"depth_bounds": [0, 2300], "time": "2020-01-01 to 2020-12-31 (daily)"` which is **5.88GB** I used the default Argo mission parameters: full cycle duration of 10 days, descend to the 1000m parking depth, drift for ~9 days, dive to 2000m, then ascend to the surface and report a profile, recording the **profile position** at the shallowest point of each cycle. Below is the profiling behavior of one of the virtual floats over its first cycles. Note, because the position of the virtual floats is written every 6 hours of simulation time the virtual float's record that is at 2000m is not recorded, this does not affect the ohc 0-2000m computation. ::: {#cell-fig-cycle-schematic .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} _ = plot_depth_profiles( TRAJ, floats=[23], max_days=22, mark_profiles=True, ncols=1, title = "Virtual float mission", show=True, ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![One float over two profiling cycles](pilot_simulation_files/figure-html/fig-cycle-schematic-output-1.png){#fig-cycle-schematic width=855 height=404} ::: ::: ::: {#run_trajsim_cli .cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code code-summary="Run the simulation (shown for reference; already executed)"} # From the repo root, in the `virtualfleet` conda env: # # $ python code/run_trajsim.py --config code/configs/nac_gs_1yr_wide.json # # Output: data/virtualfleet/nac_gs_1yr_wide_task000.zarr # "wall_seconds": 69.98, # "peak_memory_mb": 1652.6, ``` ::: ::: {#load-and-compute .cell execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code code-summary="Load trajectories, compute true OHC field and virtual-Argo profiles"} traj = open_trajectories(TRAJ) theta = xr.open_mfdataset(VELOCITY).sel( latitude=slice(TRUTH_BOUNDS[0], TRUTH_BOUNDS[1]), longitude=slice(TRUTH_BOUNDS[2], TRUTH_BOUNDS[3]), ) # True OHC on the native 1/12° grid (per day), and synthetic-Argo OHC per profile. truth_field_full = ohc.truth_ohc_field(theta).compute() sim_full = ohc.float_ohc(traj, theta) # Restrict the analysis to the deployment region (where the floats start). truth_field, sim = ohc_bias.subset_region(truth_field_full, sim_full, ANALYSIS_BOUNDS) ref = ohc.truth_domain_mean(truth_field, weighted=True) truth_by_deg = {deg: ohc.coarsen_truth(truth_field, deg=deg) for deg in DEGS} float_by_deg = {deg: ohc.grid_cells(sim, ["ohc_700", "ohc_2000"], deg=deg) for deg in DEGS} # Per-depth colour limits (from the ½° truth), shared across all OHC tabs. VLIM = {d: (float((truth_by_deg[0.5][d] * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ).quantile(0.02)), float((truth_by_deg[0.5][d] * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ).quantile(0.98))) for d in DEPTHS} ``` ::: # Analysis region The analysis domain sits on the **Gulf Stream / North Atlantic Current front** — a sharp north–south OHC gradient. The 2020-mean GLORYS OHC below shows high ohc (~90 GJ m⁻²) to the south and low ohc (~45 GJ m⁻²) to the north. **OHC definition.** For every 1/12° grid cell, OHC (GJ m⁻²) is the integral $\text{OHC} = \int_0^{z} \rho\, c_p\, T\, dz$ computed using the trapezoidal rule for $z = 700$ m and $z = 2000$ m where $T$ is the sea water potential temperature in °C reported at 50 standard depth levels (41 levels from 0m-2250m: 0.41m, 1.54m, ..., 644m, 763m, ..., 1942m, 2225m). Unlike Levitus et al. [2012], which computed $\rho$ and $c_p$ from annual climatological T/S fields, here both are fixed constants -- adequate at this study's scope (a single year, a $4°\times6°$ region, and a *bias* between two estimators computed the same way, so most of the T/S-dependence common to both cancels) but worth revisiting if the analysis later targets absolute OHC magnitudes or multi-year trends, where Levitus-style climatological $\rho,c_p$ would matter more. ::: {#cell-fig-ohc-field-2020 .cell execution_count=7} ``` {.python .cell-code} ohc_2020 = truth_field_full.mean("time") # annual mean of the daily true OHC field _ = map_fields_row( [ohc_2020["ohc_700"] * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ, ohc_2020["ohc_2000"] * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ], titles=["ohc 700m", "ohc 2000m"], cmaps=["viridis", "viridis"], cbar_labels=["GJ m$^{{-2}}$", "GJ m$^{{-2}}$"], extent=[-76, -47, 28, 50], boxes=[(*TRUTH_BOUNDS, "advection domain", '0.5', "-"), (*ANALYSIS_BOUNDS, "analysis domain")], suptitle="GLORYS 1/12° 2020-mean" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![GLORYS 1/12° 2020-mean OHC (0–700 m and 0–2000 m)](pilot_simulation_files/figure-html/fig-ohc-field-2020-output-1.png){#fig-ohc-field-2020 width=1220 height=508} ::: ::: The floats are advected by the GLORYS velocity at the ~1000 m parking depth. The 2020-mean `uo`/`vo` near 1000 m below show the strong North Atlantic Gulf Stream current ::: {#cell-fig-velocity-1000 .cell execution_count=8} ``` {.python .cell-code} u1000 = theta["uo"].sel(depth=1000, method="nearest") v1000 = theta["vo"].sel(depth=1000, method="nearest") zlev = float(u1000.depth) u1000, v1000 = u1000.mean("time"), v1000.mean("time") vlim = float(np.nanpercentile( np.abs(np.concatenate([u1000.values.ravel(), v1000.values.ravel()])), 99)) _ = map_fields_row( [u1000, v1000], titles=[f"uo (eastward), ~{zlev:.0f} m", f"vo (northward), ~{zlev:.0f} m"], cmaps=["RdBu_r", "RdBu_r"], cbar_labels=["m s$^{-1}$", "m s$^{-1}$"], vmin=-vlim, vmax=vlim, extent=[-76, -47, 28, 50], boxes=[(*TRUTH_BOUNDS, "advection domain", '0.5', "-"), (*ANALYSIS_BOUNDS, "analysis domain")], suptitle="GLORYS 2020-mean velocity near the 1000 m parking depth", ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![GLORYS 2020-mean horizontal velocity near the 1000 m parking depth.](pilot_simulation_files/figure-html/fig-velocity-1000-output-1.png){#fig-velocity-1000 width=1224 height=502} ::: ::: # Float advection trajectories ## virtual argo floats ::: {#cell-fig-trajectories .cell execution_count=9} ``` {.python .cell-code} _ = map_trajectories_minimal( TRAJ, extent=[-76, -47, 28, 50], boxes=[(*TRUTH_BOUNDS, "advection domain", '0.5', "-"), (*ANALYSIS_BOUNDS, "analysis domain")], title="Virtual Argo floats advected by GLORYS, 2020", show=True, cmap = "Dark2" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![GLORYS advected float trajectories (2020). deployment positions.](pilot_simulation_files/figure-html/fig-trajectories-output-1.png){#fig-trajectories width=808 height=562} ::: ::: Virtual floats that advect outside of the advection domain are lost to the simulation. 18 of the 24 floats stay inside the advection domain through 2020. ::: {#cell-fig-trajectories-month .cell execution_count=10} ``` {.python .cell-code} _ = map_positions_by_month( TRAJ, extent=[-76, -47, 28, 50], boxes=[(*TRUTH_BOUNDS, "advection domain", '0.5', "-"), (*ANALYSIS_BOUNDS, "analysis domain")], title="Virtual Argo floats advected by GLORYS, 2020 end of month positions", cmap = "Dark2" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![GLORYS advected end of month virtual float positions](pilot_simulation_files/figure-html/fig-trajectories-month-output-1.png){#fig-trajectories-month width=801 height=615} ::: ::: ## real argo floats There are 20 real float in `data/argo_ohc.csv` that entered the analysis domain at any point in 2020. ::: {#fig-real-argo-traj .cell execution_count=11} ``` {.python .cell-code} argo_all = pd.read_csv("../data/argo_ohc.csv") argo_all["date"] = pd.to_datetime(argo_all["date"], format="ISO8601") la0, la1, lo0, lo1 = ANALYSIS_BOUNDS ids = argo_all.loc[argo_all.lat.between(la0, la1) & argo_all.lon.between(lo0, lo1) & (argo_all["date"].dt.year == 2020), "float_id"].unique() argo_a = argo_all[argo_all.float_id.isin(ids)] _ = map_point_trajectories( argo_a, extent=[-76, -47, 28, 50], boxes=[(*TRUTH_BOUNDS, "advection domain", '0.5', "-"), (*ANALYSIS_BOUNDS, "analysis domain")], title=f"All obs for floats that profiled domain in 2020", ) argo_a = argo_a[(argo_a["date"].dt.year==2020)] _ = map_point_trajectories( argo_a, extent=[-76, -47, 28, 50], boxes=[(*TRUTH_BOUNDS, "advection domain", '0.5', "-"), (*ANALYSIS_BOUNDS, "analysis domain")], title=f"2020 obs", ) # Real Argo OHC in the analysis box (2020), gridded like the synthetic floats; real_cells_by_deg = {deg: ohc.grid_cells(argo_a, ["ohc_700", "ohc_2000"], deg=deg) for deg in DEGS} real_by_deg = {deg: real_cells_by_deg[deg] .groupby("month", as_index=False)[["ohc_700", "ohc_2000"]].mean() for deg in DEGS} ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![Real Argo floats (2020)](pilot_simulation_files/figure-html/fig-real-argo-traj-output-1.png){#fig-real-argo-traj-1 width=725 height=566} ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](pilot_simulation_files/figure-html/fig-real-argo-traj-output-2.png){#fig-real-argo-traj-2 width=725 height=566} ::: ::: ## floats in the analysis domain over time ::: {#cell-fig-sim-vs-real-argo .cell execution_count=12} ``` {.python .cell-code} sim_counts = ohc_bias.monthly_float_counts(sim).rename( columns={ "n_floats": "n_floats_virtual", "n_profiles": "n_profiles_virtual", } ) real_counts = ohc_bias.monthly_float_counts(argo_a).rename( columns={ "n_floats": "n_floats_real", "n_profiles": "n_profiles_real", } ) comp = sim_counts.merge(real_counts, on="month", how="outer") comp ``` ::: {#fig-sim-vs-real-argo .cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=10} ```{=html}

``` floats per month in the analysis domain ::: ::: # OHC Anomaly **Climatology source.** `data/rg_climatology/RG_ArgoClim_Temperature_2019.nc` (the [RG Argo Climatology](https://sio-argo.ucsd.edu/RG_Climatology.html)) contains `ARGO_TEMPERATURE_MEAN`: a single **static** mean temperature field (Jan 2004 -- Dec 2018, no seasonal/monthly dimension) on a 1° lat/lon grid and 58 pressure levels (2.5--1975 dbar). Its longitude is 0--360°E and is converted to -180..180° on load. The twelve monthly extension files `RG_ArgoClim_2020{01-12}_2019.nc` each contain the observed `ARGO_TEMPERATURE_ANOMALY` from the 2004--208 mean for each calendar month of 2020. **Climatological OHC field.** Either mean-temperature field is integrated over depth with the same trapezoidal rule used for GLORYS (`ohc.profile_ohc`, treating RG's pressure in dbar as meters, the same approximation already used throughout this report), giving an `ohc_700`/`ohc_2000` field on RG's native 1° grid. **Linear interpolation from 1° to 1/12°.** Because the true GLORYS temperature field is on a 1/12° grid, `code/ohc_climatology.py` bilinearly interpolates the climatological ohc to the 1/12° grid for each month. **Anomaly.** For a profile or grid cell at $x = (x_{lat}, x_{lon})$ observed at day $t$, the OHC anomaly is calculated by subtracting the spatially interpolated OHC to the matching calendar month that grid cell was observed in from the observed daily OHC value. The same anomaly is added back to the climatology after GP interpolation (see below) to recover an absolute-OHC field. ::: {#climatology-prep .cell execution_count=13} ``` {.python .cell-code code-summary="Load the RG climatology (seasonal + static) and compute the truth OHC anomaly"} import ohc_climatology as ohc_clim mean_da = ohc_clim.load_rg_mean() ext_da = ohc_clim.load_rg_extensions(year=2020) # 12 monthly 2020 anomalies vs 2004-2018 mean clim_field_A = ohc_clim.climatological_ohc_field(mean_da + ext_da).compute() clim_field_B = ohc_clim.climatological_ohc_field(mean_da) # static 2004-2018 mean clim_field = clim_field_A # used throughout the rest of the report # GLORYS truth OHC anomaly (native 1/12°), in the analysis region. truth_anom = ohc_clim.truth_ohc_anomaly(truth_field, clim_field) truth_anom_cells = ohc_clim.to_cells_table(truth_anom) # Shared diverging colour range for every *anomaly* map below ANOM_VLIM = {d: float(truth_anom_cells[d].abs().quantile(0.98)) * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ for d in DEPTHS} ``` ::: Below is comparison of the RG climatology published temperature trend and my recreation ![Published RG Climatology global-mean temperature time series (source: sio-argo.ucsd.edu)](../data/rg_climatology/Temperature_2019.png){width=50%} ::: {#cell-fig-rg-timeseries-check .cell execution_count=14} ``` {.python .cell-code code-summary="Reconstruct published RG global-mean temperature time series (2004–2026)"} import matplotlib.dates as mdates from pathlib import Path _ds_rg = xr.open_dataset(ohc_clim.RG_MEAN_PATH, decode_times=False) _mean_v = _ds_rg["ARGO_TEMPERATURE_MEAN"].values # (pres, lat, lon) _anom_v = _ds_rg["ARGO_TEMPERATURE_ANOMALY"].values # (180, pres, lat, lon) _p = _ds_rg["PRESSURE"].values _lat = _ds_rg["LATITUDE"].values _ds_rg.close() _dp = np.diff(_p) _coslat = np.cos(np.deg2rad(_lat)) def _vol_mean_T(T): T_mid = (T[:-1] + T[1:]) / 2 # trapezoidal averages; NaN where either endpoint NaN w = _dp[:, None, None] * _coslat[None, :, None] return float(np.nansum(T_mid * w) / np.nansum(np.where(np.isnan(T_mid), 0.0, w))) _dates_main = pd.date_range("2004-01", periods=180, freq="MS") _tmean_main = np.array([_vol_mean_T(_mean_v + _anom_v[i]) for i in range(180)]) # Load all available extension months (2019 onward) _ext_targets = [(y, m) for y in range(2019, 2027) for m in range(1, 13) if not (y == 2026 and m > 2)] _ext_dates, _ext_vals = [], [] for _y, _m in _ext_targets: _path = f"../data/rg_climatology/RG_ArgoClim_{_y}{_m:02d}_2019.nc" if not Path(_path).exists(): continue _ds = xr.open_dataset(_path, decode_times=False) _anom = _ds["ARGO_TEMPERATURE_ANOMALY"].values.squeeze(0) _ext_vals.append(_vol_mean_T(_mean_v + _anom)) _ext_dates.append(pd.Timestamp(_y, _m, 1)) _ds.close() # Bridge the Dec 2018 / Jan 2019 gap by prepending the last climatology point # to the extension series so the two line segments share an endpoint. _bridge_date = pd.Timestamp(2018, 12, 1) _bridge_val = _tmean_main[-1] _all_dates = np.array([_bridge_date] + _ext_dates, dtype="datetime64[ns]") _all_vals = np.concatenate([[_bridge_val], _ext_vals]) _smooth = pd.Series( np.concatenate([_tmean_main, _ext_vals]), ).rolling(12, center=True, min_periods=12).mean().values _sm_dates = np.array(list(_dates_main) + _ext_dates, dtype="datetime64[ns]") _sm_ok = ~np.isnan(_smooth) fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 4)) ax.plot(_dates_main, _tmean_main, color="black", lw=0.8, label="2004–2018 (mean + anom)") ax.plot(_all_dates, _all_vals, color="red", lw=0.8, label=f"2019–{_ext_dates[-1].year} extensions") ax.plot(_sm_dates[_sm_ok], _smooth[_sm_ok], color="limegreen", lw=1, label="12-month running mean") ax.set_ylabel("Potential Temperature (°C)") ax.set_xlim(_dates_main[0], _all_dates[-1]) ax.xaxis.set_major_locator(mdates.YearLocator(2)) ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter("%Y")) ax.grid(alpha=0.2) fig.tight_layout() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![Verification: global-mean RG potential temperature reconstructed from mean + monthly extension anomalies. The ~0.04 °C upward offset vs the published plot above is expected — the RG website excludes marginal seas and the Arctic from its spatial average; we include all non-NaN cells.](pilot_simulation_files/figure-html/fig-rg-timeseries-check-output-1.png){#fig-rg-timeseries-check width=853 height=373} ::: ::: ::: {#fig-climatology-map-static .cell execution_count=15} ``` {.python .cell-code code-summary="RG climatology (static), native 1° grid vs interpolated to 1/12°"} _clim_B_interp = ohc_clim.climatology_on_grid( clim_field_B, truth_field_full["latitude"].values, truth_field_full["longitude"].values ) _STATIC_MAP_KW = dict( titles=["ohc 700m", "ohc 2000m"], cmaps=["viridis", "viridis"], cbar_labels=["GJ m$^{{-2}}$", "GJ m$^{{-2}}$"], extent=[-76, -47, 28, 50], boxes=[(*TRUTH_BOUNDS, "advection domain", '0.5', "-"), (*ANALYSIS_BOUNDS, "analysis domain")], ) _ = map_fields_row( [clim_field_B["ohc_700"] * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ, clim_field_B["ohc_2000"] * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ], suptitle="RG climatology (native 1°)", **_STATIC_MAP_KW, ) _ = map_fields_row( [_clim_B_interp["ohc_700"] * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ, _clim_B_interp["ohc_2000"] * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ], suptitle="RG climatology (bilinear -> 1/12°)", **_STATIC_MAP_KW, ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](pilot_simulation_files/figure-html/fig-climatology-map-static-output-1.png){#fig-climatology-map-static-1 width=1220 height=508} ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](pilot_simulation_files/figure-html/fig-climatology-map-static-output-2.png){#fig-climatology-map-static-2 width=1220 height=508} ::: ::: ::: {#fig-climatology-map-seasonal .cell execution_count=16} ``` {.python .cell-code code-summary="RG climatology by month, native 1° grid vs interpolated to 1/12°"} _clim_A_months = [pd.Timestamp(2020, m, 1) for m in clim_field_A["month"].values] _clim_A_plot = clim_field_A.sel( latitude=slice(TRUTH_BOUNDS[0] - 2, TRUTH_BOUNDS[1] + 2), longitude=slice(TRUTH_BOUNDS[2] - 2, TRUTH_BOUNDS[3] + 2), ).assign_coords(month=_clim_A_months) _clim_A_native_cells = ohc_clim.to_cells_table(_clim_A_plot) _clim_A_interp_cells = ohc_clim.climatology_cells_table( clim_field_A, truth_field_full["latitude"].values, truth_field_full["longitude"].values, months=_clim_A_months, ) _clim_bounds = { d: (float((_clim_A_native_cells[d] * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ).quantile(0.02)), float((_clim_A_native_cells[d] * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ).quantile(0.98))) for d in DEPTHS } def show_clim_map(kind, d): cmin, cmax = _clim_bounds[d] if kind == "native": return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps( _clim_A_native_cells, d, vmin=cmin, vmax=cmax, width=TABSET_FIGW, title=f"RG climatology {d} by month (native 1°)") if kind == "interp": return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps( _clim_A_interp_cells, d, vmin=cmin, vmax=cmax, width=TABSET_FIGW, title=f"RG climatology {d} by month (bilinear -> 1/12°)") if kind == "anom": return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps( truth_anom_cells, d, vmin=-ANOM_VLIM[d], vmax=ANOM_VLIM[d], cmap="RdBu_r", cbar_label=f"{d} anomaly (GJ m$^{{-2}}$)", width=TABSET_FIGW, title=f"GLORYS truth - RG climatology (native 1/12°)") _emit("::: {.panel-tabset}") for d in DEPTHS: _emit(f"## {DEPTH_LABEL[d]}") _emit("::: {.panel-tabset}") for kind, label in [("native", "Native 1°"), ("interp", "Interpolated to 1/12°"), ("anom", "Anomaly")]: _emit(f"### {label}") _show_fig(show_clim_map(kind, d)) _emit(":::") _emit(":::") ``` ::: {.panel-tabset} ## 0–700 m ::: {.panel-tabset} ### Native 1°

The predicted anomaly field is added back to the climatological mean to give an absolute-OHC field directly comparable to the GLORYS truth on the same native grid. `code/ohc_gp_interp.R` fits one GP per depth (0-700m, 0-2000m) with the stationary spatiotemporal covariance function `matern_spheretime`, pooling all ~150 profiles across the year, with covariance parameters $\theta$ = ($\sigma^2$, $\rho_s$, $\rho_t$, $\nu$, $\tau^2$) = (variance, spatial range, temporal range, smoothness, nugget). The `matern_spheretime` function embeds the (lat, lon) into 3D coordinates, and then inputs the resulting locations into the `matern_spacetime` covariance function wtih parameterization $$ k_{\theta}(x, y) = \sigma^2\,\frac{2^{1-\nu}}{\Gamma(\nu)} (\|D^{-1}(x-y)\|)^\nu\,K_\nu\!\left(\|D^{-1}(x-y)\|\right) + \sigma^2\tau^2\,\mathbf{1}_{x = y}, $$ where $D = \operatorname{diag}(\rho_s,\rho_s,\rho_s,\rho_t)$ and $K_\nu$ is the modified Bessel function of the second kind. Smoothness is fixed at $\nu = \tfrac{3}{2}$, such that the function is once differentiable. *Note, If the smoothness parameter is free the Fisher scoring algorithm does not converge.* When $\nu = \tfrac{3}{2}$, the covariance function has the closed form $(1+r)\,e^{-r}$ where $r = \|D^{-1}(s-s')\|$. ::: {.callout-note collapse="true"} ## Derivation: Matérn $\nu=\tfrac{3}{2}$ closed form The general Matérn covariance (dropping $\sigma^2$ and the nugget) is $$ k(r) = \frac{2^{1-\nu}}{\Gamma(\nu)}\,r^{\nu}\,K_{\nu}(r), \qquad r \geq 0. $$ For $\nu = p + \tfrac{1}{2}$ with $p$ a non-negative integer, $K_\nu$ reduces to an elementary function via the identity $$ K_{p+1/2}(r) = \sqrt{\frac{\pi}{2r}}\,e^{-r}\sum_{k=0}^{p}\frac{(p+k)!}{k!\,(p-k)!}\,(2r)^{-k}. $$ Setting $p = 1$ (so $\nu = \tfrac{3}{2}$) gives $K_{3/2}(r) = \sqrt{\tfrac{\pi}{2r}}\,e^{-r}(1 + r^{-1})$, i.e.\ $K_{3/2}(r) = \sqrt{\tfrac{\pi}{2}}\,r^{-3/2}\,e^{-r}(r+1)$. Substituting into the general form: $$ k(r) = \frac{2^{1-3/2}}{\Gamma(3/2)}\,r^{3/2}\cdot\sqrt{\tfrac{\pi}{2}}\,r^{-3/2}\,e^{-r}(1+r) = \frac{2^{-1/2}}{\tfrac{\sqrt{\pi}}{2}}\cdot\frac{\sqrt{\pi}}{\sqrt{2}}\,e^{-r}(1+r). $$ Therefore $$ \boxed{k(r) = (1+r)\,e^{-r}}, $$ where $r = \|D^{-1}(s-s')\|$. ::: ## Model Fit `GpGp::fit_model` fits the mean parameter $\hat\beta$ (`fit$betahat`) and the `matern_spheretime` covariance parameters. Asymptotic standard errors from `fit$info` (log scale) are transformed back to natural scale with the delta method: ${\mathrm{se}}(\theta) \approx \theta\, {\mathrm{se}}(\log\theta)$. ::: {#tbl-gp-fit-diagnostics .cell execution_count=18} ``` {.python .cell-code code-summary="Per-depth fit diagnostics: log-likelihood, convergence, profile count"} sim_anom = ohc_clim.float_ohc_anomalies(sim, clim_field) _GP_DIR = "../data/gp_interp" os.makedirs(_GP_DIR, exist_ok=True) _profiles_csv = f"{_GP_DIR}/profiles_anom.csv" _fit_summary_csv = f"{_GP_DIR}/gp_fit_summary.csv" _model_cache = f"{_GP_DIR}/gp_model_cache.rds" # fitted model shared across grids _keys = ["month", "cell_lat", "cell_lon"] _truth_monthly = truth_field.resample(time="1MS").mean().rename({"time": "month"}) truth_native = truth_by_deg[1 / 12] # Climatological mean field on the native grid -- reused by Levitus tabsets below. mean_field_cells = ohc_clim.climatology_cells_table( clim_field, truth_field["latitude"].values, truth_field["longitude"].values, months=_truth_monthly["month"].values, ) # Temporal prediction point within each month: "first" (1st), "middle" (15th), "last" (last day). _GP_MONTH_DAY = "middle" # Prediction resolutions -- add entries here to predict on additional grids. _GP_RES_LABELS = {1/12: "1/12° (native)", 0.5: "1/2°", 1.0: "1°"} _pred_grid_csvs = {deg: f"{_GP_DIR}/pred_grid_{deg:.4g}deg.csv" for deg in [1/12, 1.0]} _virt_out_csvs = {deg: f"{_GP_DIR}/gp_predictions_{deg:.4g}deg.csv" for deg in [1/12, 1.0]} def _grid_lats_lons(deg): if deg < 0.1: return truth_field["latitude"].values, truth_field["longitude"].values return (np.sort(truth_by_deg[deg]["cell_lat"].unique()), np.sort(truth_by_deg[deg]["cell_lon"].unique())) for deg, grid_csv in _pred_grid_csvs.items(): lats, lons = _grid_lats_lons(deg) ohc_clim.build_pred_grid(lats, lons).to_csv(grid_csv, index=False) ohc_clim.write_profile_csv(sim_anom, _profiles_csv) # First iteration fits the model and saves it to cache; subsequent iterations load it. for deg, out_csv in _virt_out_csvs.items(): ohc_clim.run_gp_interp( _profiles_csv, _pred_grid_csvs[deg], out_csv, _fit_summary_csv, model_cache=_model_cache, month_day=_GP_MONTH_DAY, ) gp_fit_summary = ohc_clim.load_gp_fit_summary(_fit_summary_csv) gp_fit_summary.groupby("depth")[["loglik", "converged", "n_obs"]].first() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` loaded cached model from ../data/gp_interp/gp_model_cache.rds wrote 12 rows -> ../data/gp_interp/gp_fit_summary.csv wrote 42924 rows -> ../data/gp_interp/gp_predictions_0.08333deg.csv loaded cached model from ../data/gp_interp/gp_model_cache.rds wrote 12 rows -> ../data/gp_interp/gp_fit_summary.csv ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` wrote 420 rows -> ../data/gp_interp/gp_predictions_1deg.csv ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=16} ```{=html}

_truth_monthly[list(DEPTHS)]).rename( {d: f"{d}_bias" for d in DEPTHS} ) bias_cells = ohc_clim.to_cells_table(_bf, value_cols=[f"{d}_bias" for d in DEPTHS]) else: _merged = cells.merge( truth_by_deg[deg][_keys + list(DEPTHS)].rename(columns={d: f"{d}_truth" for d in DEPTHS}), on=_keys, how="left", ) bias_cells = _merged.copy() for _d in DEPTHS: bias_cells[f"{_d}_bias"] = bias_cells[_d] - bias_cells[f"{_d}_truth"] return dict( cells=cells, se_cells=se_cells, anom_cells=anom_cells, obs_anom_cells=obs_anom_cells, bias_cells=bias_cells, mean_cells=mean_cells, truth_cells=truth_by_deg[deg], obs_profiles=profiles_df, ) def build_gp_ctx(out_csvs, profiles_df=None): """Build the {res_label: context} dict used by emit_gp_map_tabset / emit_gp_violin_tabset.""" return {_GP_RES_LABELS[deg]: build_gp_res_ctx(out_csv, deg, profiles_df) for deg, out_csv in out_csvs.items()} _virtual_ctx = build_gp_ctx(_virt_out_csvs, profiles_df=sim_anom) ``` ::: ::: {#tbl-gp-fit-summary .cell execution_count=20} ``` {.python .cell-code code-summary="Format the pooled GP fit parameter table for display"} def _fmt_fit_summary(df): """One row per parameter, columns 'estimate ± SE' per depth, in display units.""" d = df.copy() d["display"] = d.apply( lambda r: f"{r['estimate']:.4g}" if pd.isna(r["std_error"]) else f"{r['estimate']:.4g} ± {r['std_error']:.3g}", axis=1, ) wide = d.pivot(index="parameter", columns="depth", values="display") order = ["mean (intercept)", "variance", "spatial_range", "temporal_range", "smoothness", "nugget"] return wide.reindex([p for p in order if p in wide.index]) # Spatial range: radians (sphere of radius 1) -> km. Temporal range: already in # days (day-of-year coordinate). Mean/variance/nugget: J/m2 (or squared) -> GJ/m2 # (or squared). nugget in GpGp's matern_spheretime is a *relative* fraction of # variance -- K(0) = variance * (1 + nugget) (verified directly against # matern_spheretime's output) -- so it's dimensionless, unlike variance itself; # no GJ rescaling. EARTH_RADIUS_KM = 6371.0 _scale = { "mean (intercept)": ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ, "variance": ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ ** 2, "spatial_range": EARTH_RADIUS_KM, "temporal_range": 1.0, "smoothness": 1.0, "nugget": 1.0, } _disp = gp_fit_summary.copy() _disp["estimate"] = _disp.apply(lambda r: r["estimate"] * _scale[r["parameter"]], axis=1) _disp["std_error"] = _disp.apply(lambda r: r["std_error"] * _scale[r["parameter"]], axis=1) fit_table = _fmt_fit_summary(_disp) fit_table.index.name = "parameter" fit_table ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=18} ```{=html}

``` ::: ::: **Units** mean (GJ m$^{{-2}}$), variance (GJ m$^{{-4}}$), spatial_range (km), temporal_range (days), nugget (relative fraction of variance) ## Predictions Use `GpGp`'s machinery but perform simple kriging with an estimated mean by conditioning every prediction point on *all* observed profiles, rather than on a subset of neighbors. Predictions are made at the center of grid cells on the 15th of each month (configurable via `_GP_MONTH_DAY`: `"first"`, `"middle"`, or `"last"`). ::: {.callout-note collapse="true"} ## `kriging_predict` logic Let $y \in \mathbb{R}^{n}$ be *all* of that depth's profile OHC anomalies pooled across the year, at space-time locations $s_1, \dots, s_n$ (`fit$y`, `fit$locs`), and let $s_0$ be one prediction point. `kriging_predict` sets the neighbour count to $n$ (all pooled profiles). Writing $K \in \mathbb{R}^{n\times n}$ for the observation covariance matrix $K_{ij} = k_{\hat\theta}(s_i, s_j)$, $k_0 \in \mathbb{R}^n$ for the observation-to-prediction cross-covariance $k_{0,i} = k_{\hat\theta}(s_i, s_0)$, and $k_{00} = k_{\hat\theta}(s_0, s_0)$ the prior variance at $s_0$, the classical kriging predictor is $$ \hat y(s_0) = \hat\beta + b^\top\,(y - \hat\beta \mathbf{1}_n), \qquad b = K^{-1}k_0, \qquad \widehat{\mathrm{Var}}\big[y(s_0)\big] = k_{00} - k_0^\top K^{-1} k_0 . $$ `kriging_predict` calls `Linv_all <- vecchia_Linv(covparms, covfun_name, locs_all, NNarray, start_ind)` factors for the Cholesky factorization $K = LL^\top$. For the prediction row, directly returns *both* the kriging weight vector $b$ and the conditional standard deviation $\sigma_0 = \sqrt{k_{00} - k_0^\top b}$, packed into one sparse row $$ \texttt{Linv\_all[i, ]} = \big[\,1/\sigma_0,\ -b_1/\sigma_0,\ \dots,\ -b_n/\sigma_0\,\big], $$ such that $$ b_k = -\frac{\texttt{Linv\_all[i,k+1]}}{\texttt{Linv\_all[i,1]}}, \qquad \hat y(s_0) = \hat\beta + \sum_{k=1}^n b_k\,(y_k - \hat\beta), \qquad \widehat{\mathrm{Var}}\big[y(s_0)\big] = \frac{1}{(\texttt{Linv\_all[i,1]})^2}. $$ ::: ::: {#gp-interp-maps .cell execution_count=21} ``` ::: ::: ::: # GP interpolation with real floats Now we use the real Argo float profile positions from 2020 in the analysis domain, but assign each profile the **GLORYS truth OHC anomaly** at that 1/12° cell and month. The GP then conditions on truth values at real float locations rather than virtual float locations; any difference from the virtual-Argo GP above is purely due to spatial coverage (which cells the floats happened to sample), not measurement error or model mismatch. Same fit machinery, same prediction grids, same climatology. ::: {#gp-real-coverage-prep .cell execution_count=23} ``` {.python .cell-code code-summary="Build real-Argo profile table using GLORYS truth anomalies, fit GP"} # Sample GLORYS truth OHC at each real Argo profile's position and date via # nearest-neighbour interpolation on truth_field -- exactly the same xarray # interp path used for virtual floats in float_ohc_anomalies. This avoids any # cell-snapping / float-join precision issues. _real_dates = xr.DataArray(pd.to_datetime(argo_a["date"]).values, dims="profile") _real_lats = xr.DataArray(argo_a["lat"].values, dims="profile") _real_lons = xr.DataArray(argo_a["lon"].values, dims="profile") _truth_at_real = truth_field.interp( time=_real_dates, latitude=_real_lats, longitude=_real_lons, method="nearest" ) _real_as_sim = argo_a[["lat", "lon", "date"]].copy() for _d in DEPTHS: _real_as_sim[_d] = _truth_at_real[_d].values real_argo_anom = ohc_clim.float_ohc_anomalies(_real_as_sim, clim_field) _real_profiles_csv = f"{_GP_DIR}/profiles_real_anom.csv" _real_model_cache = f"{_GP_DIR}/gp_real_model_cache.rds" _real_out_csvs = {deg: f"{_GP_DIR}/gp_real_predictions_{deg:.4g}deg.csv" for deg in [1/12, 1.0]} ohc_clim.write_profile_csv(real_argo_anom, _real_profiles_csv) for deg, out_csv in _real_out_csvs.items(): ohc_clim.run_gp_interp( _real_profiles_csv, _pred_grid_csvs[deg], out_csv, _fit_summary_csv, model_cache=_real_model_cache, month_day=_GP_MONTH_DAY, ) _real_ctx = build_gp_ctx(_real_out_csvs, profiles_df=real_argo_anom) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` loaded cached model from ../data/gp_interp/gp_real_model_cache.rds wrote 12 rows -> ../data/gp_interp/gp_fit_summary.csv ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` wrote 42924 rows -> ../data/gp_interp/gp_real_predictions_0.08333deg.csv loaded cached model from ../data/gp_interp/gp_real_model_cache.rds wrote 12 rows -> ../data/gp_interp/gp_fit_summary.csv ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` wrote 420 rows -> ../data/gp_interp/gp_real_predictions_1deg.csv ``` ::: ::: ## Predictions ::: {#gp-real-coverage-maps .cell execution_count=24} ``` {.python .cell-code code-summary="Real-Argo-coverage GP maps"} emit_gp_map_tabset(_real_ctx) ``` ::: {.panel-tabset} ## 0–700 m ::: {.panel-tabset} ### 1/12° (native) ::: {.panel-tabset} #### Mean field (climatology)

::: ::: ::: # `fields::Krig` audit To corroborate the fitted values and standard errors, `code/gp_audit_fields.R` independently reproduces the kriging predictions using `fields::Krig` with a hand-coded `matern_spheretime` correlation function that matches `GpGp`'s implementation. The fitted covariance parameters (spatial/temporal ranges, nugget) are fixed at GpGp's ML estimates; `fields::Krig` estimates $\sigma^2$ by GLS. Three prediction columns are compared: | Column | Method | Notes | |--------|--------|-------| | `pred_gpgp` | GpGp custom `kriging_predict` | primary result | | `pred_sk` | Direct Cholesky solve (R base) | same covariance, same $\hat \beta$, plug-in simple kriging | | `pred_fields` | `fields::Krig` ordinary kriging | GLS $\hat \beta$, beta uncertainty included in SE | Standard errors: `se_sk` uses GpGp's $\sigma^2$ with the simple-kriging formula (holds $\beta$ fixed); `se_fields` uses `fields`' $\sigma^2_{GLS}$ with the ordinary-kriging formula (adds $\beta$ estimation uncertainty). ::: {#cell-gp-fields-audit .cell execution_count=26} ``` {.python .cell-code code-summary="Run fields::Krig audit and display summary"} _audit_csv = f"{_GP_DIR}/gp_predictions_0.08333deg_fields_audit.csv" ohc_clim.run_gp_audit_fields( _profiles_csv, _pred_grid_csvs[1/12], _virt_out_csvs[1/12], _model_cache, _audit_csv, ) _audit = ohc_clim.load_gp_audit(_audit_csv) # predictions / SEs are in J/m² -- convert to GJ/m² for display for _c in ["pred_gpgp","pred_sk","pred_fields","se_gpgp","se_sk","se_fields", "pred_sk_diff","pred_fields_diff"]: _audit[_c] = _audit[_c] * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ # sigma2 is in J²/m⁴ -- just use the ratio (dimensionless) for the table _summary_rows = [] for _d in DEPTHS: _sub = _audit[_audit["depth"] == f"{_d}_anom"] _sigma2_ratio = float(_sub["sigma2_fields"].iloc[0] / _sub["sigma2_gpgp"].iloc[0]) _summary_rows.append({ "depth": _d, "σ² ratio (fields/GpGp)": f"{_sigma2_ratio:.6f}", "RMSE(pred_sk − pred_gpgp) (GJ/m²)": f"{(_sub['pred_sk_diff']**2).mean()**0.5:.2e}", "RMSE(pred_fields − pred_gpgp) (GJ/m²)": f"{(_sub['pred_fields_diff']**2).mean()**0.5:.4f}", "max|pred_fields − pred_gpgp| (GJ/m²)": f"{_sub['pred_fields_diff'].abs().max():.4f}", "median(se_sk / se_gpgp)": f"{(_sub['se_sk']/_sub['se_gpgp']).median():.6f}", "median(se_fields / se_gpgp)": f"{(_sub['se_fields']/_sub['se_gpgp']).median():.6f}", }) pd.DataFrame(_summary_rows).set_index("depth") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Profiles: 150 | grid cells: 3577 | months: 12 Depths in cache: ohc_700_anom, ohc_2000_anom === ohc_700_anom === GpGp: sigma2=3.683e+19 rs=0.007069 rad (~45 km) rt=5.58 days nugget=0.001447 beta=3.416e+08 n_obs = 150 fields: sigma2_GLS=3.687e+19 (ratio vs GpGp: 1.0011) beta=3.439e+08 (delta=2.36e+06) Direct SK vs GpGp custom: RMSE(pred): 0.005169 J/m2 max|diff|: 0.06111 J/m2 median(se_sk/se_gpgp): 0.999155 fields::Krig vs GpGp custom: RMSE(pred): 1.338e+06 J/m2 max|diff|: 2.347e+06 J/m2 median(se_fields/se_gpgp): 1.001962 === ohc_2000_anom === GpGp: sigma2=7.669e+19 rs=0.008281 rad (~53 km) rt=6.56 days nugget=0.001179 beta=-3.033e+08 n_obs = 147 fields: sigma2_GLS=7.671e+19 (ratio vs GpGp: 1.0002) beta=-3.064e+08 (delta=-3.089e+06) Direct SK vs GpGp custom: RMSE(pred): 0.004527 J/m2 max|diff|: 0.0488 J/m2 median(se_sk/se_gpgp): 0.999239 fields::Krig vs GpGp custom: RMSE(pred): 1.524e+06 J/m2 max|diff|: 3.039e+06 J/m2 median(se_fields/se_gpgp): 1.001229 Wrote 85848 rows -> ../data/gp_interp/gp_predictions_0.08333deg_fields_audit.csv ``` ::: ::: {#gp-fields-audit .cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=24} ```{=html}

Standard errors match within 0.1–0.2% (se_sk/se_gpgp ≈ 1.000; se_fields/se_gpgp ≈ 1.002), with the slight excess in `se_fields` expected from ordinary-kriging's added $\hat \beta$ estimation term. # Levitus-style kernel-smoother interpolation ## Background Now we perform anomaly interpolation with the fixed-radius Gaussian-weighted kernel smoother described in `literature/levitus_2012_aux.tex` (the Levitus objective-analysis scheme). The predicted anomaly is $\hat A(s_0)$ at a point $s_0$ with nearby profile anomalies $Q_n$ at distance $r_n$: $$ w_n = \exp\!\left(-E\,\frac{r_n^2}{R^2}\right) \text{ for } r_n \le R,\ \text{else } 0, \qquad E = 4, $$ $$ \hat A(s_0) = \sum_n \lambda_n Q_n, \qquad \lambda_n = \frac{w_n}{\sum_m w_m}. $$ **Radius.** Levitus sets $R = 666$ km close to the size of this *entire* ~500 km x 650 km analysis domain **Interpolation standard errors** The Levitus appendix derives a standard error for the analyzed value with a general formula for error propagation (GFEP), then simplifies using the Schwarz inequality bound on cross-covariances. Their equations appear to imply that $\sigma_A$, the standard error for an interpolated anomaly, is equal to $\sigma_0$, the standard deviation of the weighted mean anomaly values within a given influence region. I compute two standard errors, `se_O`, which assumes $Corr(C_n, C_m) = 1$ for grid points $n$ and $m$ within the same influence region, and `se_a`, which alternatively assumes $Cov(C_n, C_m) = 0$. $$ \texttt{se\_0} = \sigma_0, $$ $$ \texttt{se\_a} = \sigma_0\sqrt{\textstyle\sum_n \lambda_n^2} \qquad\text{(dropping the cross-terms)}, $$ ::: {.callout-note title="Source derivation: Levitus et al. 2012 appendix" collapse="true"} **Notation.** At prediction gridpoint $(i,j)$, let $N$ be the number of 1°-square mean anomalies $Q_n$ within the influence radius, with weights $w_n = \exp(-4\,r_n^2/R^2)$ and $W = \sum_n w_n$. Define the *correction contribution* from the $n$th square, $$ C_n = \frac{w_n\,Q_n}{W}, $$ so the analyzed anomaly is $A = F + \sum_n C_n$ (first-guess $F = 0$ for anomaly fields). **General formula for error propagation (GFEP) .** Treating $\partial A/\partial Q_n = w_n/W$: $$ \sigma_A = \sqrt{ \sum_{n=1}^{N}\!\left(\frac{w_n}{W}\,\sigma_{C_n}\right)^{\!2} +\; \frac{2}{W^2}\sum_{n=1}^{N-1}\sum_{m=n+1}^{N} w_n w_m\,\sigma_{C_{n,m}} }, $$ where $\sigma_{C_n}$ is the standard deviation of anomalies in the $n$th square and $\sigma_{C_{n,m}}$ is the cross-covariance between squares $n$ and $m$. The standard deviation ($\sigma_0$) of all observed ODSQ mean anomalies within the influence region surrounding an ODSQ at gridpoint $(i,j)$ is defined as: $$ \sigma_0 = \sqrt{\frac{1}{N-1}\sum_{n=1}^{N}\!\left(C_n - \bar{C}\right)^2}. $$ The cross covariance $\sigma_{C_{n,m}}$ in can be replaced by the quantity $\sigma_{C_n}\sigma_{C_m}$ because the Schwarz inequality guarantees that $\left|\sigma_{C_{n,m}}\right| \le \sigma_{C_n}\sigma_{C_m}$. Next we assume in equation that $\sigma_{C_n} = \sigma_0$ and we have the standard error of the mean of the objectively analyzed value: $$ \sigma_A = \frac{\sigma_0}{W}\sqrt{\sum_{n=1}^{N} w_n^2 + 2\!\sum_{n ../data/gp_interp/levitus_predictions.csv ``` ::: ::: ::: {#levitus-interp-maps .cell execution_count=28} ``` {.python .cell-code code-summary="Levitus-interpolated field vs native-grid ::: ::: # OHC Bias for Sampled Cells *For this initial analysis no interpolation of ohc was done for cells without profiles* **OHC for virtual floats.** For the profiling position of each virtual float, the full vertical column of potential temperature at the corresponding 1/12° grid cell was used to calculate the observed synthetic ohc for a given day. The ohc was than averaged to the monthly 1/12°, 1/2°, 1° grid cell level by cosine latitude weighted averages of daily 1/12° cell means. The *true* ohc from the GLORYS re-analysis was similarly aggregated from daily 1/12° to the monthly 1/2° and 1° grid cell level. **Sampling Bias** is defined as the difference in true GLORYS ohc for the virtually sampled cells and true GLORYS ohc across all cells in the analysis domain. - *Difference between the `GLORYS truth (sampled cells)` and `GLORYS truth (all cells)` series below* - *Captures the bias from preferentially sampling regions in the analysis domain* **Grid Bias** is defined as the difference in the synthetic ohc aggregated from the virtual floats profiling positions and the true GLORYS ohc for the virtuall sampled cells. - *Difference between the `synthetic Argo (sampled cells)` and `GLORYS truth (sampled cells)` series below* - *Captures the bias from averaging from the daily 1/12° level to the monthly $x$° level* **Total OHC Bias** is the sum of **Sampling Bias** and **Grid Bias** - *As cells get finer, grid bias is traded for sampling bias.* - *At the 1/12° level, grid bias is due to cells not being observed every day in a month* ::: {#maps-prep .cell execution_count=33} ``` {.python .cell-code code-summary="Grid truth / floats / difference at every resolution; load EN4"} # Bias against the fixed area-weighted reference, once per resolution; reuse its # per-cell grid error (diff) and per-month domain table (timeseries). bias_by_deg = {deg: ohc_bias.compute_bias(float_by_deg[deg], truth_by_deg[deg], true_domain_mean=ref) for deg in DEGS} diff_by_deg = {deg: bias_by_deg[deg]["cells"] for deg in DEGS} en4_cells = ohc.load_en4_cells( "../data/ohc_en4_gridded_nwatlantic.csv", bounds=ANALYSIS_BOUNDS, year=2020) en4_series = en4_cells.groupby("month", as_index=False)[["ohc_700", "ohc_2000"]].mean() # Per-depth y-range for the domain-mean timeseries, shared across resolutions # (min/max of the three lines over every deg) so the tabs line up when flipped. def _ylim(d): cols = [f"{d}_true_mean", f"{d}_true_at_sampled", f"{d}_float_mean"] parts = [bias_by_deg[deg]["domain"][cols].to_numpy().ravel() for deg in DEGS] parts += [real_by_deg[deg][d].to_numpy() for deg in DEGS] # include real Argo line v = np.concatenate(parts) * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ lo, hi = np.nanmin(v), np.nanmax(v) pad = 0.05 * (hi - lo) return (lo - pad, hi + pad) YLIM = {d: _ylim(d) for d in DEPTHS} # Figures here are emitted via "diff": dc = diff_by_deg[deg] bmax = float((dc[f"{d}_bias"].abs() * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ).quantile(0.95)) return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(dc, value_col=f"{d}_bias", vmin=-bmax, vmax=bmax, cmap="RdBu_r", cbar_label=f"{d} bias (GJ m$^{{-2}}$)", lats=glats, lons=glons, scatter_dots=dots, width=TABSET_FIGW, title=f"Grid bias, {d} (deg={deg:.3g})") if kind == "en4": return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(en4_cells, d, vmin=VLIM[d][0], vmax=VLIM[d][1], width=TABSET_FIGW, title=f"EN4 {d} by month, 2020 (1°)") def show_table(d, deg): """Monthly preferential-sampling summary table for depth d at cell size deg.""" return ohc_bias.monthly_bias_table(float_by_deg[deg], truth_by_deg[deg], d, ref) def show_timeseries(d, deg): """Return the domain-mean OHC timeseries figure (truth all / sampled / virtual Argo).""" return ohc_bias.plot_domain_timeseries(bias_by_deg[deg]["domain"], value_col=d, ylim=YLIM[d], real=real_by_deg[deg], en4=en4_series if deg == 1.0 else None, figsize=(TABSET_FIGW, TABSET_FIGH+4), title=f"Domain-mean {d} over time (deg={deg:.3g})") # --- source maps shown in each resolution tab; EN4 only exists at its native 1° --- SOURCES = [("count", "Virtual Argo Profiles"),("real_count", "Real Argo Profiles"), ("sim", "Virtual Argo ohc"),("real", "Real Argo ohc"), ("truth", "GLORYS ohc"), ("diff", "Grid Bias"), ("en4", "EN4")] ``` ::: deg in DEGS: _emit(f"### {_deg_label(deg)}") _show_fig(show_timeseries(d, deg)) #display(show_table(d, deg)) _emit("::: {.panel-tabset}") for kind, label in SOURCES: if kind == "en4" and deg != 1.0: continue # EN4 only at its native 1 deg _emit(f"#### {label}") _show_fig(show_map(kind, d, deg)) _emit(":::") # close source tabset display(show_table(d, deg)) _emit(":::") # close resolution tabset
